protocol_name: HSCT Autograft - Melphalan and Bortezomib (IV) Conditioning

drugs.notes: Infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes at 1100hrs. Dose rounded down to nearest 10mg. 90-minute expiry - must be made locally for individual patient. Requires adequate urine output >500ml since 0900hrs before administration; if <500ml give furosemide 20mg IV bolus and recheck.

Intravenous bolus over 5 seconds. Day -1 dose to be given at least 12 hours after melphalan infusion (approximately 2330hrs). Day +4 dose given as per schedule. Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands.

dose_modifications.notes: Full dose 200mg/m²
Reduce to 140mg/m²
Clinical decision - high dose melphalan not recommended. Discuss with Transplant Director or Senior Transplant Clinician.
No dose changes recommended - no information available on melphalan in hepatic impairment.
Full dose
Consider initiating treatment at 0.7mg/m²
Full dose
Clinical decision

dose_modifications.condition: Bortezomib: NCI-CTC grade 1 peripheral neuropathy (no pain)
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with full dose

dose_modifications.condition: Bortezomib: NCI-CTC grade 1 neuropathy with pain or grade 2 neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 1mg/m²
Confirm with transplant consultant before proceeding if there are signs of disease relapse.

pre_medications: Aprepitant 125mg oral once on day of melphalan, then 80mg oral once daily for 2 subsequent days
Dexamethasone 4mg oral each morning with each dose of aprepitant (3 days)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times a day until nausea subsides
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV twice a day for 10 days then review
Furosemide 20mg IV bolus at 1010hrs on day of melphalan; repeat at 1045hrs if urine output <500ml since 0900hrs
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml IV over 90 minutes starting 0930hrs on day of melphalan (pre-hydration)
Sodium chloride 0.9% with potassium chloride 27mmol 1000ml IV overnight (evening before melphalan, to complete by 0930hrs)
Nystatin mouthwash 1ml four times a day until count recovery
Sodium chloride 0.9% mouthwash 10ml four times a day until count recovery
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required as premedication for stem cells
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required as premedication for stem cells
Proton pump inhibitor (gastric protection) throughout admission
Ciprofloxacin 250mg twice daily from day +5 whilst neutropenic (per institution guideline)
Fluconazole 100mg once daily from admission until recovery from neutropenia (per institution guideline)
Aciclovir 400mg twice or three times daily from admission (per institution guideline)
Filgrastim 30 million units SC once daily from day +7 until neutrophils >0.5 (or lenograstim 33.6 million units SC from day +5) - per local formulary
Thromboprophylaxis per individual transplant schedule
special_warnings: Melphalan has a 90-minute expiry from preparation - strict adherence to administration timing is essential. Contact pharmacy at 0900hrs for delivery by 1045hrs.
Bortezomib IV on day -1 must be given at least 12 hours after melphalan infusion.
Melphalan dose rounded DOWN to nearest 10mg - do not use dose banding tables.
Urine output must be >500ml since 0900hrs before melphalan can be administered; if <500ml after two doses of furosemide 20mg IV, contact prescriber.
Melphalan - non-vesicant.

Post-melphalan hydration: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 2hrs at 1130hrs; 1000ml NaCl 0.9% + KCl 27mmol over 4hrs at 1330hrs; 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 6hrs at 1730hrs; 1000ml NaCl 0.9% + KCl 27mmol over 8hrs at 2330hrs; 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 8hrs at 0730hrs day after melphalan.
Confirm correct protocol with individual transplant schedule.

disease_site: HSCT / EBV reactivation / PTLD / Immune-mediated cytopenias

drugs.notes: Flat dose 100mg - no dose banding or rounding required. Infusion in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9%. First infusion over 120 minutes; if well tolerated, subsequent infusions may be given over 30-60 minutes. Rituximab - neutral (extravasation classification).

dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment needed
No dose adjustment needed
Rituximab is not considered myelotoxic; no dose adjustment required for haematological parameters.
Delay treatment until adverse effect resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then reduce to 75% of original dose or discontinue as appropriate.
Reduce to 75% of original dose
Stop treatment if a third dose reduction is necessary.
Permanently discontinue rituximab if PML confirmed. Suspend dosing if PML suspected until excluded.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 minutes prior to rituximab
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion-related reactions (confirm with haematologist before administering)
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab-related bronchospasm
Pethidine 25mg IV bolus when required for rituximab-related rigors not responsive to corticosteroids
special_warnings: Check hepatitis B status before starting treatment - risk of hepatitis B reactivation.

Severe cytokine release syndrome risk: characterised by severe dyspnoea, bronchospasm, hypoxia, fever, chills, rigors, urticaria, angioedema. May feature tumour lysis syndrome components. Can lead to acute respiratory failure and death. Occurs most commonly within 1-2 hours of first infusion.
Hypersensitivity reactions including anaphylaxis have been reported - emergency medications must be immediately available.
Monitor for Progressive Multifocal Leukoencephalopathy (PML) at regular intervals - any new or worsening neurological, cognitive or psychiatric symptoms.
Viral upper respiratory tract infection at time of treatment may increase risk of rituximab-associated hepatotoxicity - assess for cold/flu symptoms prior to treatment.
Infusion reactions observed in approximately 10% of patients.
All dose reductions due to adverse drug reactions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.

protocol_name: Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant - Hydration Prescription for Cyclophosphamide Chemotherapy Conditioning for HSCT

drugs.notes: Day -7 at 2200hrs: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 12 hours at 83ml/hr (Line 1, Hydration). Furosemide 20-40mg IV STAT may be required during treatment to maintain diuresis/fluid balance.

Day -6 at 1000hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% + KCl 0.2% 1000ml over 6 hours at 167ml/hr. Repeated at 1600hrs (over 6 hours at 167ml/hr) and 2200hrs (over 6 hours at 167ml/hr). Furosemide 20-40mg IV may be required to maintain fluid balance.

Day -5 at 0400hrs: 1000ml Glucose 5% over 6 hours at 167ml/hr. Also on Day -4 at 0400hrs: 1000ml Glucose 5% over 6 hours at 167ml/hr. Continued NaCl 0.9% + KCl 0.2% infusions at 1000hrs, 1600hrs, 2200hrs on day -5 at 167ml/hr each. Fluid prescription continues on normal fluid prescription sheet after day -4. TBI given days -3 to -1 (see protocol). Cyclophosphamide prescribed on ARIA; Mesna prescribed on JAC via line 2.

pre_medications: Furosemide 20-40mg IV STAT as required during treatment to maintain diuresis and fluid balance
special_warnings: This is a standalone hydration/supportive care prescription for cyclophosphamide HSCT conditioning (TBI-related donor conditioning).
Cyclophosphamide is prescribed on ARIA; Mesna is prescribed on JAC and administered via line 2.
TBI is given on days -3 to -1 - refer to separate protocol.
Fluid prescription continues on normal fluid prescription sheet after the scheduled hydration days.
Furosemide 20-40mg IV may be required to maintain diuresis and fluid balance throughout the hydration period.

HSCT conditioning protocol: dose modifications are managed by the transplant team based on day-of-treatment organ function. Do not apply standard dose modification rules — follow transplant centre guidelines.

protocol_name: Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant - Hydration Prescription for High Dose Melphalan Chemotherapy Conditioning for HSCT

drugs.notes: Pre-hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% IV started no later than midnight on day -2, to be completed by 0930hrs on day -1. Rate is variable and depends on time started. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Day -1 at 0930hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 90 minutes at 667ml/hr. Contact pharmacy at 0845hrs to request melphalan for delivery by 1045am. Start fluid balance sheet and daily weight measurement at 0900hrs. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Day -1 at 1010hrs: furosemide 20mg IV bolus over 5 minutes. At 1045hrs: measure urine output since 0900hrs. If <500ml give second dose of furosemide 20mg IV bolus and re-measure urine output before starting melphalan. If urine output <250ml since 0900hrs despite two doses of furosemide 20mg IV, contact prescriber first before administering melphalan.

Post-melphalan hydration at 1130hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 2 hours at 500ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Post-melphalan hydration at 1330hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% + KCl 0.2% (27mmol) over 4 hours at 250ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Post-melphalan hydration at 1730hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 6 hours at 166ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Post-melphalan hydration at 2330hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% + KCl 0.2% (27mmol) over 8 hours at 125ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Day after melphalan (day 0) at 0730hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 8 hours at 125ml/hr, then restart routine IV fluids. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

pre_medications: Aprepitant - prescribed on inpatient prescribing system (confirm administered by 1000hrs day -1)
Metoclopramide - prescribed on inpatient prescribing system (confirm administered by 1000hrs day -1)
Ondansetron - prescribed on inpatient prescribing system (confirm administered by 1000hrs day -1)
special_warnings: This is a standalone hydration/supportive care prescription for high dose melphalan HSCT conditioning.
Melphalan is prescribed on ARIA - confirm pharmacy has prescription and request melphalan delivery to ward by 1045am.
Melphalan must only be administered if urine output >500ml since 0900hrs; if <500ml after two doses of furosemide 20mg IV, contact prescriber before administering.
If urine output <250ml since 0900hrs despite two furosemide doses, contact prescriber FIRST.
Melphalan has a short expiry - administer at 1100hrs as scheduled.
Start fluid balance sheet and daily weight measurement from 0900hrs on day of melphalan.

HSCT conditioning protocol: dose modifications are managed by the transplant team based on day-of-treatment organ function. Do not apply standard dose modification rules — follow transplant centre guidelines.

protocol_name: Wessex Blood and Bone Marrow Transplant - Hydration Prescription for High Dose Melphalan and Velcade (Bortezomib) Chemotherapy Conditioning for HSCT

drugs.notes: Pre-hydration: 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% IV started no later than midnight on day -2, to be completed by 0930hrs on day -1. Rate is variable and depends on time started. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Day -1 at 0930hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 90 minutes at 667ml/hr. Contact pharmacy at 0845hrs to confirm melphalan prescription on ARIA and request delivery to ward by 1045am. Start fluid balance sheet and daily weight measurement at 0900hrs. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Day -1 at 1010hrs: furosemide 20mg IV bolus over 5 minutes. At 1045hrs: if urine output since 0900hrs <500ml, give second dose furosemide 20mg IV bolus and re-measure urine output before starting melphalan. If urine output <250ml despite two doses of furosemide 20mg IV, contact prescriber first.

Post-melphalan hydration at 1130hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 2 hours at 500ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Post-melphalan hydration at 1330hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% + KCl 0.2% (27mmol) over 4 hours at 250ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Post-melphalan hydration at 1730hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 6 hours at 166ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Post-bortezomib hydration at 2330hrs (concurrent with bortezomib administration): 1000ml NaCl 0.9% + KCl 0.2% (27mmol) over 8 hours at 125ml/hr. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

Day 0 at 0730hrs: 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 8 hours at 125ml/hr, then restart routine IV fluids. | Hydration given as multiple sequential timed bags throughout the day — each entry represents one scheduled infusion bag. All bags are intentional sequential hydration doses.

pre_medications: Aprepitant - prescribed on inpatient prescribing system (confirm administered by 1000hrs day -1)
Metoclopramide - prescribed on inpatient prescribing system (confirm administered by 1000hrs day -1)
Ondansetron - prescribed on inpatient prescribing system (confirm administered by 1000hrs day -1)
special_warnings: This is a standalone hydration/supportive care prescription for high dose melphalan with Velcade (bortezomib) HSCT conditioning.
Melphalan is prescribed on ARIA - confirm pharmacy has prescription and request delivery by 1045am on day -1.
Bortezomib (Velcade) is prescribed on ARIA and given at 2330hrs on day -1 (at least 12 hours after melphalan infusion at 1100hrs).
Bortezomib also given on day +4 - see ARIA prescription.
Melphalan must only be given if urine output >500ml since 0900hrs; if <500ml after two furosemide doses, contact prescriber.
If urine output <250ml despite two furosemide 20mg IV doses, contact prescriber FIRST before melphalan.
Start fluid balance sheet and daily weight measurement from 0900hrs on day -1.

HSCT conditioning protocol: dose modifications are managed by the transplant team based on day-of-treatment organ function. Do not apply standard dose modification rules — follow transplant centre guidelines.

drugs.notes: Initial dose 40mg/kg once daily. Dose calculated on actual or ideal body weight, whichever is less. Reduce by 50% to 20mg/kg/day when WBC drops below 20x10⁹/L. Adjust individually to maintain WBC 5-10x10⁹/L. Available as 500mg hard capsules; doses rounded to nearest 250mg on ARIA. 28-day cycle until disease progression or intolerance (6 cycles set in ARIA). Initial response assessed after 6 weeks. Capsules swallowed whole or contents dispersed in water immediately prior to administration. Safe handling procedures for oral chemotherapy must be followed.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 50% from 40mg/kg to 20mg/kg daily
Reduce dose; adjust individually to maintain WBC 5-10x10⁹/L
Interrupt therapy to determine whether cytopenia is treatment or disease related. If disease related, may resume at consultant haematologist discretion.
85% of dose and titrate to response
80% of dose and titrate to response
75% of dose and titrate to response
50% of dose and titrate to response
20% of dose and titrate to response
Dose reductions probably not necessary but remains clinical decision due to lack of information. Increase monitoring frequency.

dose_modifications.condition: Suspected Hydroxycarbamide syndrome (fever, hepatitis, pneumonitis within 4 weeks)
dose_modifications.notes: Stop immediately on suspicion - do not restart. High mortality condition.

pre_medications: Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily until WBC <10x10⁹/L (cycle 1 for 28 days)
special_warnings: Indication: chronic or accelerated phase CML in patients intolerant or refractory to a tyrosine kinase inhibitor.
Hydroxycarbamide syndrome: rare syndrome of fever, hepatitis and pneumonitis occurring within 4 weeks of commencing therapy - high mortality. Stop immediately and do not restart on suspicion.
Concomitant use with didanosine (with or without stavudine) is CONTRAINDICATED - increased risk of fatal and non-fatal hepatotoxicity.
Must be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist or via trust-approved shared care agreement.
Capsule contents must not be inhaled or contact skin/mucous membranes - safe handling procedures must be followed.
Follow NPSA/2008/RRR001 National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy.
Available as 500mg capsules only; 100mg and 1000mg tablets are licensed for sickle cell disease only.
Monitor FBC, U&Es and LFTs fortnightly initially, increasing to 3-monthly in stable responding patients.
Always check for drug interactions when initiating new drugs.

drugs.notes: 420mg once daily (OD) oral for all cycles (cycles 1-15). Ibrutinib lead-in monotherapy for cycles 1-3. Venetoclax added from cycle 4. Swallow tablets whole with water at approximately the same time each day. Avoid grapefruit, grapefruit juice, Seville oranges, and starfruit. Available as 140mg, 280mg, 420mg film-coated tablets. If missed dose, take as soon as possible same day; do not take extra tablets. Max 15 cycles of ibrutinib per protocol.

Cycle 4, days 1-7: 20mg OD (ramp-up week 1). Take in the morning with food. Swallow whole with water. Available as 10mg, 50mg and 100mg film-coated tablets. Venetoclax should be dispensed 24 hours prior to each dose escalation date. TLS risk assessment must be documented in ARIA before each venetoclax dose escalation. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 4, days 8-14: 50mg OD (ramp-up week 2). Take in the morning with food. TLS monitoring required at pre-dose, 6-8 hours, and 24 hours after first dose of 20mg and 50mg. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 4, days 15-21: 100mg OD (ramp-up week 3). Take in the morning with food. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 4, days 22-28: 200mg OD (ramp-up week 4). Take in the morning with food. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

Cycle 5 onwards: 400mg OD days 1-28. Take in the morning with food. Swallow whole with water. Max 12 cycles of venetoclax per protocol. If dosing interruption >1 week during first 5 weeks of dose-titration or >2 weeks after completing titration, reassess TLS risk before restarting. | RAMP-UP SCHEDULE: multiple Venetoclax entries exist for this protocol, each representing a different weekly dose in the ramp-up schedule (20mg → 50mg → 100mg → 200mg → 400mg). All entries are intentional.

dose_modifications.condition: Ibrutinib: Grade 3 neutropenia (ANC <1x10⁹/L with infection/fever) or Grade 4 haematological toxicity - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt ibrutinib. Once toxicity resolves to grade 1 or baseline, reinitiate ibrutinib at starting dose (420mg).

dose_modifications.condition: Ibrutinib: Grade 3 neutropenia or Grade 4 haematological toxicity - 2nd and subsequent occurrences
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose by 140mg to 280mg. If toxicities persist or recur following two dose reductions, discontinue ibrutinib.

dose_modifications.condition: Venetoclax: Grade 3 neutropenia or Grade 4 haematological toxicity - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt venetoclax. Consider G-CSF. Once toxicity resolves to grade 1 or baseline, venetoclax may be resumed at same dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Venetoclax: Grade 3 neutropenia or Grade 4 haematological toxicity - 2nd and subsequent occurrences
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt venetoclax. Consider G-CSF. Follow dose reduction guideline (Table 1): 400mg→300mg, 300mg→200mg, 200mg→100mg, 100mg→50mg, 50mg→20mg, 20mg→10mg. Modified dose continued for 1 week before increasing.

dose_modifications.condition: Venetoclax: Other grade 3-4 non-haematological toxicity - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt venetoclax. Once resolved to grade 1 or baseline, resume at same dose (no modification required).

dose_modifications.condition: Venetoclax: Other grade 3-4 non-haematological toxicity - 2nd and subsequent occurrences
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt venetoclax. Follow dose reduction guideline in Table 1 when resuming.

dose_modifications.condition: Venetoclax: TLS - blood chemistry changes or symptoms suggestive of TLS
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold next day's dose. If resolved within 24-48 hours, resume at same dose. If takes >48 hours to resolve, resume at reduced dose (Table 1) and discuss with consultant. If clinical TLS, resume at reduced dose after resolution.
Reduce to 280mg once daily
Reduce to 140mg once daily
Not recommended

dose_modifications.condition: Venetoclax: Hepatic impairment Child-Pugh A or B
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required
At least 50% dose reduction required. Monitor closely for signs of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Ibrutinib: Renal impairment CrCl <80 but >30 mL/min
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment necessary
No data available. Use only if benefit outweighs risks. Monitor closely for toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Venetoclax: Renal impairment CrCl <80 but >30 mL/min
dose_modifications.notes: Increased risk of TLS. More intensive TLS prophylaxis and increased TLS monitoring may be recommended.
No data available. Use only if benefit outweighs risks. Monitor closely for TLS signs and toxicity.

pre_medications: Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily for 7 days (ibrutinib initiation cycle 1); reduce to 100mg OD if CrCl <20mL/min

Allopurinol 300mg oral once daily starting 72 hours prior to initiation of venetoclax in cycle 4, for 5 weeks continuously including first week of cycle 5 (unless otherwise indicated by TLS risk assessment)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily for duration of treatment (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral once daily on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday for duration of treatment (PCP prophylaxis)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take-home antiemetic, cycle 1 only)
Oral loperamide 4mg at onset of diarrhoea, then 2-4mg after each loose stool (maximum 16mg/24 hours)
Oral hydration: 1.5-2L water daily, 2 days prior to and on days of venetoclax dose initiation and each dose increase
Rasburicase 3mg IV over 30 minutes in 50mL NaCl 0.9% - for high TLS risk patients on day 1 of cycle 1 and day 8 if uric acid raised (omit allopurinol on days of rasburicase)
G-CSF per local formulary if clinically indicated (filgrastim 30 million units SC OD, lenograstim 33.6 million units SC OD, or pegfilgrastim 6mg SC once)
special_warnings: FIXED DURATION: Ibrutinib up to maximum 15 cycles; venetoclax up to maximum 12 cycles.

TLS RISK: High risk with venetoclax. TLS risk assessment MUST be documented in ARIA journal before cycles 1 and 4. Patients at high baseline TLS risk due to tumour bulk should be re-assessed with CT imaging before cycle 4.
Venetoclax ramp-up schedule in cycle 4: 20mg (days 1-7) → 50mg (days 8-14) → 100mg (days 15-21) → 200mg (days 22-28) → 400mg (cycle 5 onwards days 1-28).
TLS monitoring: Outpatient for low risk (pre-dose, 6-8hrs, 24hrs for first 20mg and 50mg doses); Moderate risk outpatient/ambulatory; High risk ambulatory/inpatient.
Ibrutinib and venetoclax are primarily metabolized by CYP3A4. Check for CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers and P-gp/BCRP substrates/inhibitors - dose modifications may be required.
Avoid grapefruit, grapefruit juice, Seville oranges, and starfruit (carambola) during treatment.
Ibrutinib must be withheld at least 3-7 days pre- and post-surgery (bleeding risk). Consider stopping venetoclax with ibrutinib for surgery.
Cases of ventricular tachyarrhythmia reported - temporarily discontinue ibrutinib if symptoms of ventricular arrhythmia develop. Assess benefit-risk before restarting.
Consider changing ACE inhibitors/ARBs to alternative treatments given possible risk of sudden death.
Confirm immunophenotype or histology prior to therapy. Record TLS risk - CT scan (neck, chest, abdomen, pelvis).
Genetic tests for 17p deletion, TP53 mutation, and IGHV mutation status required.
Hepatitis B, hepatitis C, HIV 1+2 status required before starting treatment.
If patient discontinues either drug for reasons other than disease progression, the other drug should be continued unless directed otherwise by treating consultant.
Follow NPSA/2008/RRR001 National Patient Safety Alert on Oral Systemic Anti-Cancer Therapy.

drugs.notes: 420mg once daily oral, days 1-28. 28-day cycle until disease progression or intolerance (12 cycles set in ARIA). Tablets swallowed whole with water at approximately the same time each day. Available as 140mg, 280mg and 420mg tablets. Dose rounded to nearest 140mg (up if halfway). Avoid grapefruit, grapefruit juice, and Seville oranges.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity: ANC <1x10⁹/L with infection/fever, OR neutrophils <0.5x10⁹/L, OR platelets <30x10⁹/L - 1st occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment. Restart at 420mg once toxicity has resolved to grade 1.
Restart at 280mg once daily after recovery to grade 1.
Restart at 140mg once daily after recovery to grade 1.
Discontinue ibrutinib.
Reduce to 280mg once daily. Monitor for signs of toxicity.
Reduce to 140mg once daily. Monitor for signs of toxicity.
Not recommended.
No dose adjustment required.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment CrCl <30 mL/min or dialysis
dose_modifications.notes: No data available. Prescribe only if benefit outweighs risk; monitor carefully for toxicity.

pre_medications: Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral once daily on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday (anti-infective prophylaxis, for duration of treatment)
special_warnings: NICE TA429 indication: CLL in adults with at least one prior therapy, or with 17p deletion or TP53 mutation as first-line where chemo-immunotherapy is unsuitable.

Ibrutinib is a BTK inhibitor metabolized primarily by CYP3A4. Caution with agents affecting coagulation or platelet function, or influencing CYP3A4 - check for all drug interactions when initiating new drugs.
Ibrutinib must be withheld at least 3-7 days pre- and post-surgery depending on type of surgery and bleeding risk.
Bleeding risk: caution with concurrent anticoagulants, antiplatelet agents, or NSAIDs.
Atrial fibrillation and ventricular tachycardia risk - monitor cardiac status.
Hypertension risk - monitor blood pressure.
Hepatitis B reactivation - check hepatitis B status before starting treatment.
Avoid grapefruit, grapefruit juice, and Seville oranges while on ibrutinib.
Must be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist or oncologist.
Follow NPSA/2008/RRR001 National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy.
Monitor FBC, U&Es and LFTs prior to starting, then every 28 days for first 12 weeks, thereafter every 12 weeks if counts stable.
